front: Architect: co-founded OMA (Office of Metropolitan Architecture) with Elia Zenghelis
Architect: winner of the 2000 Pritzker Prize
Architect: Zaha Hadid studied under him at the Architectural Association in London
Architect: Harvard professor who coined the term 'Junkspace'
Architect: OMA designed the Fondazione Prada in Milan and a 'Transformer' multipurpose building for Prada in Seoul
Architect: OMA designed the Qatar National Library in Education City, Doha (completed 2017)
Architect: designed De Rotterdam — the largest building in the Netherlands
Architect: designed the McCormick Tribune Campus Center at IIT, topped by a huge tubular metal train tunnel
Architect: designed the Shenzhen Stock Exchange building
Architect: designed the RAK Convention Center in the UAE, which resembles the Death Star
Architect: designed the Guggenheim Hermitage Museum in Las Vegas
Architect: OMA worked on the Serpentine Gallery pavilion in London (2006)
Building: glass-and-steel public library in Seattle featuring an irregular diamond-patterned facade
Building: contains a four-story 'spiral' — a continuous book ramp with Dewey numbers 000–999 painted on the floor
Building: its Mixing Chamber is connected to the Living Room by fluorescent yellow-green escalators
Building: meeting rooms connected by ring-shaped corridors painted blood red
Building: contains George Legrady's installation 'Making Visible the Invisible' — six plasma screens showing live data
Building: conceived as five stable platforms connected by four 'in between' planes
Building: two slanted towers connected by a 75-meter perpendicular cantilevered section forming a loop
Building: Beijing structure often nicknamed for its resemblance to a pair of shorts
Building: designed with Ole Scheeren for China Central Television
Book: introduced the concept of 'Manhattanism' — an ideology embracing urban congestion
Book: subtitled 'A Retroactive Manifesto for Manhattan'; called the city the 'twentieth century's Rosetta Stone'
Book: cover features wife Madelon Vriesendorp's painting 'Flagrant Délit' — two distorted skyscrapers in bed next to the Statue of Liberty's torch
Book: co-designed with Bruce Mau; entries arranged within categories Small, Medium, Large, and Extra-Large
Book: contains the essay 'Islam after Einstein' and the poem 'Learning Japanese'; describes a Yokohama masterplan as 'programmatic lava'
Book: develops the concept of the 'Architecture of Bigness'
Building: 2002 never-adopted redesign of the EU flag that visually resembles a barcode; praised for easy updating with new member states
Building: concert hall in Porto, Portugal with an angular white concrete form compared to a Jawa sandcrawler
Building: designed in collaboration with Cecil Balmond; a 'moving house' for a client in a wheelchair, in Bordeaux
